items.mistake: Confusing singular values with eigenvalues of A directly
items.fix: Remember σᵢ are sqrt(eigenvalues of AᵀA); eigenvalues of A itself differ unless A is symmetric.
items.why: For non-symmetric A, eigenvalues can be complex while singular values are always real and non-negative.
items.example: A = [[0, 1], [-1, 0]] has eigenvalues ±i but singular values [1, 1].

items.mistake: Running PCA without centering the data first
items.fix: Subtract column means before SVD when the goal is covariance-style PCA.
items.why: Uncentered SVD finds directions of maximal raw variance including the offset from the origin, which is usually not intended.
items.example: If all points cluster near (100, 100), uncentered first component may point toward the mean offset rather than spread within the cluster.

items.mistake: Using too many or too few components by habit (e.g. always k=2)
items.fix: Choose k from explained variance, reconstruction error on validation data or downstream task performance.
items.why: Fixed k ignores spectrum shape; under-truncation loses signal, over-truncation fits noise.
items.example: Plot cumulative variance `cumsum(s**2)/sum(s**2)` and pick smallest k above 90% threshold for a dashboard embedding.
